seoTitle: Gray vs Great Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gray and Great Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gray vs Great Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gray and Great Bay.
heading: Gray vs Great Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gray, Tasmania and Great Bay, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Great Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Gray (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gray or Great Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gray has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Great Bay scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gray scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Great Bay scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gray or Great Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Great Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
